content: The performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and dedicated curious agents can be resolved by integrating 2024-2025 SOTA active learning memory architectures that prioritize storage of only high-uncertainty exploratory trajectories, rather than encoding all encountered state transitions. This targeted memory pruning for curiosity-driven RL creates a more sample-efficient curious agent that outperforms both traditional ICM/RND setups and undirected curious agents without structured active memory gating.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
